Work in `/home/polaris/wt/faithoff`. - deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# 3-MODEL INDEPENDENT POST-MORTEM — why the RACE "fixes" degraded, how to unwire them, how to do it pre-generation instead
2	
3	You are ONE of three independent reviewers (Sol / Fable / K3). Produce YOUR OWN verdict — do not defer to the others. Be blunt, specific, line-level. The orchestrator (Claude) built fixes that DEGRADED the score; your job is a merciless post-mortem + a correct pre-generation redesign.
4	
5	## THE HARD-WON PRINCIPLE (operator, 2026-07-22) — this frames everything
6	1. **NO post-generation fixes. EVER.** Any code that edits/merges/rewrites/repairs/filters the report AFTER the compose LLM has written it BREAKS comprehensiveness and readability. This is the same failure family as the "faithfulness-ghost" that cost this project months. The LLM's generated prose is more coherent than any post-hoc regex/surgery.
7	2. **All intelligence goes PRE-generation.** There are exactly TWO legitimate places for a fix:
8	   (a) the SEARCH / RETRIEVAL scope contract — controls WHAT evidence enters the pipeline;
9	   (b) the COMPOSE LLM PROMPT — controls HOW the model writes (format, style, structure, emphasis, transitions, no repetition, well-formed tables).
10	   Nothing after the model has written.
11	3. **Constraints belong in the SEARCH phase as a smart scope contract — NOT as a subtractive filter on a fixed corpus.** Example: "only cite journal articles" must NOT be done by deleting non-journal sources after generation, and must NOT even be done by subtracting from a pre-built 997-row corpus at compose time (that just thins 997→127 → degrade). Instead the scope contract, DURING search, (a) pushes away unwanted source types AND (b) keeps searching deeper/broader to find MORE of the wanted source types — so the generation input is BOTH compliant AND MORE comprehensive (end with 300+ wanted sources, not 127 survivors of a cut). Subtraction thins; an exclude-and-deepen search contract grows the right evidence base.
12	4. **Readability/format/style = a robust COMPOSE PROMPT**, so the model generates the wanted format natively — never post-hoc reformatting.
13	
14	## WHAT HAPPENED (the degrade — facts)
15	- Baseline/champion RACE (task 72, DeepResearch-Bench, comparative, weights Insight .32 / Comprehensiveness .29 / Instruction-Following .25 / Readability .14): **0.5084**.
16	- Pre-fix draw: 0.4962 (Comp .5072 / Insight .5079 / IF .4821 / Read .4690).
17	- After building 4 "fixes", every full-arm draw DEGRADED: 0.4983, 0.4769, 0.4945 — all below 0.5084.
18	- Clean draw per-dim: Comp .5023 / Insight .4977 / IF **.4981 (up)** / Read .4635. Pattern: Instruction-Following rose, but Insight/Comprehensiveness/Readability all fell → net degrade.
19	
20	## THE 4 "FIXES" TO REVIEW (identify which are post-generation = doomed)
21	1. **Narrative consolidation** — `src/polaris_graph/generator/narrative_consolidation.py` (+ wiring in `multi_section_generator.py` ~12436, ~13876). Runs AFTER sections generated; merges "duplicate" sentences in the final text. Had a decimal-regex corruption bug (later patched). POST-GENERATION.
22	2. **Cleaned-output guard** — `src/polaris_graph/generator/cleaned_output_guard.py` (compose ~611). Repairs the final report before writing. POST-GENERATION.
23	3. **Exclusive citation eligibility** — `src/polaris_graph/retrieval/exclusive_citation_eligibility.py` (+ compose ~311-570). SUBTRACTS non-journal sources from a pre-built corpus at compose-planning time (997→127 eligible → thinner report). Not post-generation strictly, but it is SUBTRACTIVE on a fixed corpus, not a search-phase contract.
24	4. **Coverage obligations** — `src/polaris_graph/generator/coverage_obligations.py` (threads role guidance into outline pre-gen + a post-gen audit + render fixup). Mostly pre-generation (the good part).
25	
26	Also review: the degraded report artifacts under `outputs/race_7phase*/**/draw_*/report.md` + `compose_summary.json`; the forensic at `/tmp/.../scratchpad/sol_forensic_verdict.txt` if accessible (Sol/Fable only — it found the consolidation regex corrupted decimal sentences, and attributed the ~43% word shrink 14384→8240 mostly to the eligibility subtraction).
27	
28	## ANSWER THESE THREE (YOUR independent verdict)
29	**Q1 — WHY did we fuck up?** Root-cause the degrade. Which of the 4 fixes caused which dimension to fall, and WHY (evidence: code lines, log/score deltas)? Confirm or challenge the thesis that post-generation manipulation + subtractive corpus filtering is the core error. Name anything else.
30	**Q2 — HOW to UNWIRE the fucked-up fixes.** For EACH post-generation / subtractive fix: exactly what to remove/disable (file:line, flag), and how to do it cleanly without breaking the pipeline (default-off is not enough if the design is wrong — say what to delete vs keep). Which of the 4 survive (and in what reduced pre-generation form)?
31	**Q3 — HOW to do it PRE-generation instead.** Concrete design:
32	   - The SEARCH-phase **scope contract** that replaces eligibility: how does it exclude unwanted source types AND drive the retriever deeper to find MORE wanted sources (so comprehensiveness GROWS)? Name the retrieval components/files it must hook into. How is it general (prompt-derived, no hardcoded venue lists, works across ~100 tasks)?
33	   - The **compose-prompt** changes that replace consolidation + cleaned-output-guard: what prompt instructions make the model natively produce non-repetitive, well-structured, well-formatted (valid tables), emphasis-rich prose?
34	   - How to lift the HIGH-WEIGHT dims (Insight .32 + Comprehensiveness .29 = 0.61) via retrieval + prompt, since those are where the points are.
35	
36	## OUTPUT FORMAT (terse, evidence-first)
37	`REVIEWER: <Sol|Fable|K3>`
38	`Q1 ROOT CAUSE:` ranked causes w/ evidence.
39	`Q2 UNWIRE PLAN:` per-fix remove/keep/reduce, file:line + flag.
40	`Q3 PRE-GENERATION REDESIGN:` scope-contract design + compose-prompt design + high-weight-dim strategy.
41	`BIGGEST RISK / DISAGREEMENT:` where you think the orchestrator or the other reviewers might be wrong.
42	Do not rubber-stamp. If the whole "beat 0.5084 by these levers" framing is wrong, say so.

You are ONE of three independent reviewers (Sol / Fable / K3). Produce YOUR OWN verdict — do not defer to the others. Be blunt, specific, line-level. The orchestrator (Claude) built fixes that DEGRADED the score; your job is a merciless post-mortem + a correct pre-generation redesign.

## THE HARD-WON PRINCIPLE (operator, 2026-07-22) — this frames everything
1. **NO post-generation fixes. EVER.** Any code that edits/merges/rewrites/repairs/filters the report AFTER the compose LLM has written it BREAKS comprehensiveness and readability. This is the same failure family as the "faithfulness-ghost" that cost this project months. The LLM's generated prose is more coherent than any post-hoc regex/surgery.
2. **All intelligence goes PRE-generation.** There are exactly TWO legitimate places for a fix:
   (a) the SEARCH / RETRIEVAL scope contract — controls WHAT evidence enters the pipeline;
   (b) the COMPOSE LLM PROMPT — controls HOW the model writes (format, style, structure, emphasis, transitions, no repetition, well-formed tables).
   Nothing after the model has written.
3. **Constraints belong in the SEARCH phase as a smart scope contract — NOT as a subtractive filter on a fixed corpus.** Example: "only cite journal articles" must NOT be done by deleting non-journal sources after generation, and must NOT even be done by subtracting from a pre-built 997-row corpus at compose time (that just thins 997→127 → degrade). Instead the scope contract, DURING search, (a) pushes away unwanted source types AND (b) keeps searching deeper/broader to find MORE of the wanted source types — so the generation input is BOTH compliant AND MORE comprehensive (end with 300+ wanted sources, not 127 survivors of a cut). Subtraction thins; an exclude-and-deepen search contract grows the right evidence base.
4. **Readability/format/style = a robust COMPOSE PROMPT**, so the model generates the wanted format natively — never post-hoc reformatting.

## WHAT HAPPENED (the degrade — facts)
- Baseline/champion RACE (task 72, DeepResearch-Bench, comparative, weights Insight .32 / Comprehensiveness .29 / Instruction-Following .25 / Readability .14): **0.5084**.
- Pre-fix draw: 0.4962 (Comp .5072 / Insight .5079 / IF .4821 / Read .4690).
- After building 4 "fixes", every full-arm draw DEGRADED: 0.4983, 0.4769, 0.4945 — all below 0.5084.
- Clean draw per-dim: Comp .5023 / Insight .4977 / IF **.4981 (up)** / Read .4635. Pattern: Instruction-Following rose, but Insight/Comprehensiveness/Readability all fell → net degrade.

## THE 4 "FIXES" TO REVIEW (identify which are post-generation = doomed)
1. **Narrative consolidation** — `src/polaris_graph/generator/narrative_consolidation.py` (+ wiring in `multi_section_generator.py` ~12436, ~13876). Runs AFTER sections generated; merges "duplicate" sentences in the final text. Had a decimal-regex corruption bug (later patched). POST-GENERATION.
2. **Cleaned-output guard** — `src/polaris_graph/generator/cleaned_output_guard.py` (compose ~611). Repairs the final report before writing. POST-GENERATION.
3. **Exclusive citation eligibility** — `src/polaris_graph/retrieval/exclusive_citation_eligibility.py` (+ compose ~311-570). SUBTRACTS non-journal sources from a pre-built corpus at compose-planning time (997→127 eligible → thinner report). Not post-generation strictly, but it is SUBTRACTIVE on a fixed corpus, not a search-phase contract.
4. **Coverage obligations** — `src/polaris_graph/generator/coverage_obligations.py` (threads role guidance into outline pre-gen + a post-gen audit + render fixup). Mostly pre-generation (the good part).

Also review: the degraded report artifacts under `outputs/race_7phase*/**/draw_*/report.md` + `compose_summary.json`; the forensic at `/tmp/.../scratchpad/sol_forensic_verdict.txt` if accessible (Sol/Fable only — it found the consolidation regex corrupted decimal sentences, and attributed the ~43% word shrink 14384→8240 mostly to the eligibility subtraction).

## ANSWER THESE THREE (YOUR independent verdict)
**Q1 — WHY did we fuck up?** Root-cause the degrade. Which of the 4 fixes caused which dimension to fall, and WHY (evidence: code lines, log/score deltas)? Confirm or challenge the thesis that post-generation manipulation + subtractive corpus filtering is the core error. Name anything else.
**Q2 — HOW to UNWIRE the fucked-up fixes.** For EACH post-generation / subtractive fix: exactly what to remove/disable (file:line, flag), and how to do it cleanly without breaking the pipeline (default-off is not enough if the design is wrong — say what to delete vs keep). Which of the 4 survive (and in what reduced pre-generation form)?
**Q3 — HOW to do it PRE-generation instead.** Concrete design:
   - The SEARCH-phase **scope contract** that replaces eligibility: how does it exclude unwanted source types AND drive the retriever deeper to find MORE wanted sources (so comprehensiveness GROWS)? Name the retrieval components/files it must hook into. How is it general (prompt-derived, no hardcoded venue lists, works across ~100 tasks)?
   - The **compose-prompt** changes that replace consolidation + cleaned-output-guard: what prompt instructions make the model natively produce non-repetitive, well-structured, well-formatted (valid tables), emphasis-rich prose?
   - How to lift the HIGH-WEIGHT dims (Insight .32 + Comprehensiveness .29 = 0.61) via retrieval + prompt, since those are where the points are.

## OUTPUT FORMAT (terse, evidence-first)
`REVIEWER: <Sol|Fable|K3>`
`Q1 ROOT CAUSE:` ranked causes w/ evidence.
`Q2 UNWIRE PLAN:` per-fix remove/keep/reduce, file:line + flag.
`Q3 PRE-GENERATION REDESIGN:` scope-contract design + compose-prompt design + high-weight-dim strategy.
`BIGGEST RISK / DISAGREEMENT:` where you think the orchestrator or the other reviewers might be wrong.

1	FAITH-GHOST / SECRET DROP: PRESENT
2	
3	Definitive but small. The 43% shrink is NOT a strict-verify/faithfulness drop: post-run resolved state has PG_STRICT_VERIFY_OFF=1 and PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=off; compose_summary + measurement.log say 9/9 sections kept, total_sentences_dropped=0, and every section logs dropped=0. Post has MORE verified units than pre (101 vs 69).
4	
5	The real ghost is narrative consolidation's sentence regex. narrative_consolidation.py:12-14 splits decimals at the period. In the actual report, two distinct full sentences ended with the same fragment `3%) by mid-2050 [19].`; consolidate_sections treated that fragment as an exact sentence duplicate, moved both [19] occurrences to the first fragment, and deleted the second fragment at lines 100-110. Artifact proof: report.md:42 now has malformed `13.6–28.**3%) by mid-2050**[18][19][19].`; report.md:106 is truncated to `13.6–28. That analysis...`. A direct replay reproduces telemetry `{consolidated: 1, evidence_multiset_preserved: true, register_rewritten: true}` while changing two distinct sentences to `13.6–28.**3%)...` and `10–28.`. Thus citation preservation is real but insufficient to guarantee content preservation.
6	
7	WORD-DROP ATTRIBUTION:
8	
9	- Total: 14,384 -> 8,240 = -6,144 words.
10	- Eligibility is dominant, but “just eligibility” is too strong. Mechanically attributable floor: about 3,852 words (63%): References shrink 1,782 -> 562 (-1,220; 139 -> 45 entries), plus routed/constructed evidence-surface expansion shrinks from +3,308 words over raw section drafts to +676 (-2,632). Logs corroborate the pool effect: 997 -> 127 planning rows and route-all orphan baskets 967 -> 106. Constructed-table surface falls 3,274 -> 854 words (-2,420), contained in that downstream bucket.
11	- Raw LLM section drafts also shrink 8,972 -> 6,716 (-2,256). Eligibility likely causes a substantial share because each writer sees the 127-row eligible pool, but it is not causally isolatable from the simultaneous prompt/wiring change at multi_section_generator.py:4099: PG_BASKET_SYNTHESIS=1 now selects the CONCISE template and adds target-free synthesis guidance. Honest eligibility estimate: at least 3,852 and plausibly about 5,000-6,100 words of the 6,144 loss; it is the dominant cause, not a proven 100% cause.
12	- Narrative consolidation: exactly one removed occurrence, 3 words (`3%) by mid-2050`), but high readability damage because it corrupts two decimal-range sentences.
13	- Cleaned-output guard: 0 words. compose_summary has cleaned_output_defects_repaired=[]; repair_for_cleaning returned the report unchanged. No table-to-prose conversion fired.
14	- Other observed delta: limitations -34 words; title/preamble/heading residue about -2. The remaining uncertain share is raw-generation variance plus the newly concise/basket prompt behavior (bounded by the -2,256 raw-draft delta), not a hidden post-generation filter.
15	
16	CONSOLIDATION SAFE?: NO
17	
18	It does not intentionally cluster different signatures, and `consolidated: 1` is the number of actual removal operations (line 110), not a report-wide undercount here. But `_SENTENCE_RE` misidentifies a decimal suffix as a whole sentence, so it removes content from a non-duplicate full sentence. The Counter assertion at lines 73 and 116-120 genuinely checks the complete citation-occurrence multiset and would revert on marker loss; the artifact passed it. It does not assert word/text preservation, so the ghost survives.
19	
20	REGISTER-REWRITE GONE?: YES
21	
22	Repo-wide search finds no `_REGISTER_REWRITES` or `_replace_register`. No register rewrite runs. `register_rewritten` at narrative_consolidation.py:124-126 is a stale/misnamed flag meaning only “any section text changed from original.” It is true because consolidation mutated text; it is not evidence of a register rewrite.
23	
24	ELIGIBILITY CONTENT COST:
25	
26	Eligibility acts before generation, not as a post-generation sentence stripper: compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:317-320 passes only body_evidence; :332-373 remaps clusters/groups; multi_section_generator.py:9362-9383 and :11813-11829 bound evidence, plan ev_ids, live_corpus, pool, and bibliography. Therefore already-generated verified sentences removed by this path = 0.
27	
28	Relative to the pre report, only 17/139 cited sources are eligible; 122/139 are excluded from the new planning/citation surface. Using the consolidator's sentence parser and bibliography-ID mapping, pre has 66 citation-bearing units: 37 units/860 words rely solely on ineligible sources and cannot survive unchanged; 16 units/369 words mix eligible+ineligible support and must lose support or be rewritten; only 13 units/267 words are wholly eligible. So 53/66 pre cited units are affected, but calling all 53 “removed” would be false: post regenerates 99 citation-bearing units (101 generator-verified units) from 45 eligible cited sources. The cost is narrower claim/source diversity and less constructed/breadth prose, plausibly reducing Insight; the upside is the observed Instruction-Following increase.
29	
30	ANY UNINTENDED SILENT DROP:
31	
32	- PRESENT: narrative_consolidation.py:12-14, 79-110 — decimal-fragment false duplicate; actual corruption at post report.md:42 and :106. Three words removed, semantic sentence broken.
33	- No other silent content-removal path found in the four modules. coverage_obligations.py only augments focus, audits, and renders all planned headings; planned_section_disclosures=[] and evidence_pack_coverage is 127/127 with missing=0. cleaned_output_guard did not fire. exclusive eligibility's 870 withheld rows are explicit in telemetry + citation_eligibility_disclosures.md, though it deliberately removes them from body planning.
34	- Wiring removals checked: legacy PG_SYNTHESIS_MATRIX generation was removed, but the flag was OFF in both runs, so zero artifact effect. The old writer-menu cap was removed (widening, not thinning). PG_INCLUDE_RESIDUAL_SECTION=1 in both; no residual render filter fired. No truncating `[:N]`/pop/filter in the four modules beyond explicit eligibility and exact-duplicate consolidation.
35	
36	READABILITY REGRESSION CAUSE:
37	
38	Best evidence-based explanation is a combination, not word count alone: (1) one conspicuous corrupted numeric sentence (`13.6–28. That analysis`) and malformed bold/citation seam from consolidation; (2) much denser walls of text—post has only 10 blank-line paragraphs versus 32 pre, despite the same 9 main sections and 38 `###` tokens; most `###` headings remain inline rather than creating clean paragraph transitions; (3) substantially less evidence/table surface (45 vs 139 cited sources; table words 854 vs 3,274), reducing comparative texture and insight opportunities; and (4) the new concise-template/basket prompt can shorten connective exposition. Coverage obligations preserved all 9 headings and added no gap stubs, so they are not a thinning mechanism. Eligibility explains most breadth loss and the Instruction-Following gain; consolidation's tiny ghost and paragraph/transition structure better explain why Readability still fell.
39

The Counter assertion at lines 73 and 116-120 genuinely checks the complete citation-occurrence multiset and would revert on marker loss; the artifact passed it. It does not assert word/text preservation, so the ghost survives.

REGISTER-REWRITE GONE?: YES

Repo-wide search finds no `_REGISTER_REWRITES` or `_replace_register`. No register rewrite runs. `register_rewritten` at narrative_consolidation.py:124-126 is a stale/misnamed flag meaning only “any section text changed from original.” It is true because consolidation mutated text; it is not evidence of a register rewrite.

ELIGIBILITY CONTENT COST:

Eligibility acts before generation, not as a post-generation sentence stripper: compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:317-320 passes only body_evidence; :332-373 remaps clusters/groups; multi_section_generator.py:9362-9383 and :11813-11829 bound evidence, plan ev_ids, live_corpus, pool, and bibliography. Therefore already-generated verified sentences removed by this path = 0.

Relative to the pre report, only 17/139 cited sources are eligible; 122/139 are excluded from the new planning/citation surface. Using the consolidator's sentence parser and bibliography-ID mapping, pre has 66 citation-bearing units: 37 units/860 words rely solely on ineligible sources and cannot survive unchanged; 16 units/369 words mix eligible+ineligible support and must lose support or be rewritten; only 13 units/267 words are wholly eligible. So 53/66 pre cited units are affected, but calling all 53 “removed” would be false: post regenerates 99 citation-bearing units (101 generator-verified units) from 45 eligible cited sources. The cost is narrower claim/source diversity and less constructed/breadth prose, plausibly reducing Insight; the upside is the observed Instruction-Following increase.

ANY UNINTENDED SILENT DROP:

- PRESENT: narrative_consolidation.py:12-14, 79-110 — decimal-fragment false duplicate; actual corruption at post report.md:42 and :106. Three words removed, semantic sentence broken.
- No other silent content-removal path found in the four modules. coverage_obligations.py only augments focus, audits, and renders all planned headings; planned_section_disclosures=[] and evidence_pack_coverage is 127/127 with missing=0. cleaned_output_guard did not fire. exclusive eligibility's 870 withheld rows are explicit in telemetry + citation_eligibility_disclosures.md, though it deliberately removes them from body planning.
- Wiring removals checked: legacy PG_SYNTHESIS_MATRIX generation was removed, but the flag was OFF in both runs, so zero artifact effect. The old writer-menu cap was removed (widening, not thinning). PG_INCLUDE_RESIDUAL_SECTION=1 in both; no residual render filter fired. No truncating `[:N]`/pop/filter in the four modules beyond explicit eligibility and exact-duplicate consolidation.

READABILITY REGRESSION CAUSE:

Best evidence-based explanation is a combination, not word count alone: (1) one conspicuous corrupted numeric sentence (`13.6–28. That analysis`) and malformed bold/citation seam from consolidation; (2) much denser walls of text—post has only 10 blank-line paragraphs versus 32 pre, despite the same 9 main sections and 38 `###` tokens; most `###` headings remain inline rather than creating clean paragraph transitions; (3) substantially less evidence/table surface (45 vs 139 cited sources; table words 854 vs 3,274), reducing comparative texture and insight opportunities; and (4) the new concise-template/basket prompt can shorten connective exposition. Coverage obligations preserved all 9 headings and added no gap stubs, so they are not a thinning mechanism. Eligibility explains most breadth loss and the Instruction-Following gain; consolidation's tiny ghost and paragraph/transition structure better explain why Readability still fell.

1	"""Final-surface narrative consolidation without changing faithfulness verdicts."""
2	from __future__ import annotations
3	
4	from collections import Counter
5	import logging
6	import re
7	from typing import Any, Sequence
8	
9	from src.polaris_graph.settings import resolve
10	
11	_OFF = frozenset({"", "0", "false", "no", "off", "disabled"})
12	_CITATION_RE = re.compile(r"\[\d+\]")
13	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.narrative_consolidation")
14	
15	# A sentence boundary is deliberately conservative: a terminator must be followed by the
16	# end of the text or whitespace and an uppercase/opening character.  Fixed-width negative
17	# lookbehinds protect common abbreviations and initials; the digit checks protect decimals.
18	_SENTENCE_RE = re.compile(
19	    r".*?(?:"
20	    r"(?:(?<!\b(?i:et\ al))(?<!\b(?i:e\.g))(?<!\b(?i:i\.e))"
21	    r"(?<!\b(?i:vs))(?<!\b(?i:fig))(?<!\b(?i:no))(?<!\b(?i:dr))"
22	    r"(?<!\b(?i:mr))(?<!\b(?i:ms))(?<!\b(?i:mrs))(?<!\b(?i:prof))"
23	    r"(?<!\b(?i:eq))(?<!\b(?i:inc))(?<!\b(?i:ltd))(?<!\b(?i:u\.s))"
24	    r"(?<!\b(?i:u\.k))(?<!\b[A-Z])"
25	    r"(?<!\d)\.(?!\d)|[!?])"
26	    r"[)\]}\"'\u2019\u201d]*(?:\s*\[\d+\])*"
27	    r"(?=\s+(?:[*_]*[A-Z]|[(\[{\"'\u2018\u201c]\s*[*_]*[A-Z])|\s*$)"
28	    r"|$)",
29	    re.DOTALL,
30	)
31	
32	
33	def enabled() -> bool:
34	    return (resolve("PG_NARRATIVE_CONSOLIDATION") or "").strip().lower() not in _OFF
35	
36	
37	def thread_narrative_guidance(plans: Sequence[Any]) -> None:
38	    """Ask the verified writer for cited relational transitions before generation."""
39	    if not enabled():
40	        return
41	    guidance = (
42	        "Use cohesive scholarly prose. For adjacent cited findings, explicitly explain with their "
43	        "citations why they agree, differ, or condition one another; emphasize the key finding or "
44	        "term with Markdown bold; describe evidence limitations through publication type, "
45	        "representativeness, and risk of bias rather than implementation vocabulary."
46	    )
47	    for plan in plans:
48	        plan.focus = f"{plan.focus.rstrip()} {guidance}"
49	
50	
51	def _signature(sentence: str) -> str:
52	    """Return exact normalized prose, excluding citation markers only."""
53	    plain = _CITATION_RE.sub(" ", sentence)
54	    plain = " ".join(plain.split())
55	    return re.sub(r"\s+([.!?])", r"\1", plain)
56	
57	
58	def _citations(text: str) -> list[str]:
59	    return _CITATION_RE.findall(text)
60	
61	
62	def _sentence_spans(text: str) -> list[tuple[str, int, int]]:
63	    """Return conservative full-sentence spans without separator whitespace."""
64	    spans: list[tuple[str, int, int]] = []
65	    for match in _SENTENCE_RE.finditer(text):
66	        raw = match.group(0)
67	        sentence = raw.strip()
68	        if not sentence:
69	            continue
70	        start = match.start() + len(raw) - len(raw.lstrip())
71	        end = match.end() - (len(raw) - len(raw.rstrip()))
72	        spans.append((sentence, start, end))
73	    return spans
74	
75	
76	def _sentence_text_multiset(texts: Sequence[str]) -> Counter[str]:
77	    return Counter(
78	        signature
79	        for text in texts
80	        for sentence, _, _ in _sentence_spans(text)
81	        if (signature := _signature(sentence))
82	    )
83	
84	
85	def _merge_citations(sentence: str, markers: Sequence[str]) -> str:
86	    """Combine markers while preserving every non-citation byte of the kept sentence."""
87	    inserted = False
88	
89	    def replace_marker(_: re.Match[str]) -> str:
90	        nonlocal inserted
91	        if inserted:
92	            return ""
93	        inserted = True
94	        return "".join(markers)
95	
96	    return _CITATION_RE.sub(replace_marker, sentence)
97	
98	
99	def _apply_sentence_edits(text: str, edits: Sequence[tuple[int, int, str]]) -> str:
100	    for start, end, replacement in sorted(edits, reverse=True):
101	        text = f"{text[:start]}{replacement}{text[end:]}"
102	    return text
103	
104	
105	def consolidate_sections(sections: Sequence[Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
106	    """Mutate rendered section text, merging exact repeated findings into one statement.
107	
108	    The canonical statement receives every citation occurrence from every repeated instance.
109	    Distinct wording is never clustered, so qualifiers and conditions remain separate. If the
110	    report-wide citation or sentence-text multiset changes unexpectedly, all section text is
111	    restored. Only complete sentences with identical normalized prose can be removed.
112	    """
113	    if not enabled():
114	        return {}
115	    active = [
116	        section for section in sections
117	        if not getattr(section, "dropped_due_to_failure", False)
118	        and not getattr(section, "is_gap_stub", False)
119	        and str(getattr(section, "verified_text", "") or "").strip()
120	    ]
121	    originals = {id(section): section.verified_text for section in active}
122	    before = Counter(marker for section in active for marker in _citations(section.verified_text))
123	    occurrences: dict[str, list[tuple[Any, str, int, int]]] = {}
124	    for section in active:
125	        text = section.verified_text
126	        if "|" in text:
127	            continue
128	        for sentence, start, end in _sentence_spans(text):
129	            if not _citations(sentence) or "\n#" in sentence:
130	                continue
131	            signature = _signature(sentence)
132	            if signature:
133	                occurrences.setdefault(signature, []).append((section, sentence, start, end))
134	
135	    consolidated = 0
136	    removed_texts: Counter[str] = Counter()
137	    edits: dict[int, list[tuple[int, int, str]]] = {id(section): [] for section in active}
138	    for signature, group in occurrences.items():
139	        if len({id(item[0]) for item in group}) < 2:
140	            continue
141	        canonical_section, canonical_sentence, canonical_start, canonical_end = max(
142	            group, key=lambda item: (len(_citations(item[1])), -list(active).index(item[0]), -item[2])
143	        )
144	        all_markers = [marker for _, sentence, _, _ in group for marker in _citations(sentence)]
145	        edits[id(canonical_section)].append(
146	            (canonical_start, canonical_end, _merge_citations(canonical_sentence, all_markers))
147	        )
148	        canonical_used = False
149	        for section, sentence, start, end in group:
150	            if (
151	                section is canonical_section
152	                and start == canonical_start
153	                and sentence == canonical_sentence
154	                and not canonical_used
155	            ):
156	                canonical_used = True
157	                continue
158	            edits[id(section)].append((start, end, ""))
159	            removed_texts[signature] += 1
160	            consolidated += 1
161	
162	    for section in active:
163	        section.verified_text = _apply_sentence_edits(
164	            originals[id(section)], edits[id(section)]
165	        )
166	
167	    after = Counter(marker for section in active for marker in _citations(section.verified_text))
168	    input_texts = _sentence_text_multiset(list(originals.values()))
169	    expected_texts = input_texts.copy()
170	    expected_texts.subtract(removed_texts)
171	    expected_texts += Counter()
172	    output_texts = _sentence_text_multiset(
173	        [section.verified_text for section in active]
174	    )
175	    try:
176	        if after != before:
177	            raise AssertionError("citation multiset changed")
178	        if output_texts != expected_texts:
179	            raise AssertionError("sentence-text multiset changed")
180	    except AssertionError as exc:
181	        for section in active:
182	            section.verified_text = originals[id(section)]
183	        logger.warning(
184	            "Narrative consolidation reverted because a preservation assertion failed: %s",
185	            exc,
186	        )
187	        return {
188	            "consolidated": 0,
189	            "consolidation_applied": False,
190	            "reverted": True,
191	            "evidence_multiset_preserved": after == before,
192	            "text_multiset_preserved": output_texts == expected_texts,
193	        }
194	    return {
195	        "consolidated": consolidated,
196	        "consolidation_applied": consolidated > 0,
197	        "evidence_multiset_preserved": True,
198	        "text_multiset_preserved": True,
199	    }
200

1	"""Structural acceptance guard for Markdown passed through benchmark cleaning."""
2	from __future__ import annotations
3	
4	from dataclasses import dataclass
5	import re
6	from typing import Sequence
7	
8	from src.polaris_graph.settings import resolve
9	
10	_OFF = frozenset({"", "0", "false", "no", "off", "disabled"})
11	_SEPARATOR_CELL_RE = re.compile(r"^:?-{3,}:?$")
12	
13	
14	@dataclass(frozen=True)
15	class TableDefect:
16	    line_number: int
17	    reason: str
18	
19	
20	def enabled() -> bool:
21	    return (resolve("PG_CLEANED_OUTPUT_GUARD") or "").strip().lower() not in _OFF
22	
23	
24	def _cells(line: str) -> list[str]:
25	    return [cell.strip() for cell in line.strip().strip("|").split("|")]
26	
27	
28	def find_malformed_tables(markdown: str) -> list[TableDefect]:
29	    lines = markdown.splitlines()
30	    defects: list[TableDefect] = []
31	    in_table = False
32	    width = 0
33	    for position, line in enumerate(lines):
34	        stripped = line.strip()
35	        if position + 1 < len(lines) and "|" in stripped:
36	            separator = _cells(lines[position + 1])
37	            if separator and all(_SEPARATOR_CELL_RE.match(cell) for cell in separator):
38	                width = len(_cells(stripped))
39	                in_table = True
40	                if len(separator) != width:
41	                    defects.append(TableDefect(position + 2, "separator_width"))
42	                continue
43	        if not in_table:
44	            continue
45	        if not stripped:
46	            in_table = False
47	            width = 0
48	            continue
49	        if not stripped.startswith("|"):
50	            defects.append(TableDefect(position + 1, "multiline_cell"))
51	            in_table = False
52	            width = 0
53	            continue
54	        if len(_cells(stripped)) != width:
55	            defects.append(TableDefect(position + 1, "row_width"))
56	    return defects
57	
58	
59	def _table_to_prose(lines: Sequence[str]) -> str:
60	    rows = [_cells(line) for line in lines if "|" in line]
61	    if len(rows) < 2:
62	        return " ".join(line.strip() for line in lines if line.strip())
63	    headers = rows[0]
64	    data = [row for row in rows[2:] if len(row) == len(headers)]
65	    sentences: list[str] = []
66	    for row in data:
67	        parts = [f"{header}: {value}" for header, value in zip(headers, row) if header and value]
68	        if parts:
69	            sentences.append("; ".join(parts) + ".")
70	    continuation = [line.strip() for line in lines if "|" not in line and line.strip()]
71	    return " ".join(sentences + continuation)
72	
73	
74	def repair_for_cleaning(markdown: str) -> tuple[str, list[TableDefect]]:
75	    if not enabled():
76	        return markdown, []
77	    defects = find_malformed_tables(markdown)
78	    if not defects:
79	        return markdown, []
80	    lines = markdown.splitlines()
81	    output: list[str] = []
82	    position = 0
83	    while position < len(lines):
84	        if position + 1 < len(lines) and "|" in lines[position]:
85	            separator = _cells(lines[position + 1])
86	            if separator and all(_SEPARATOR_CELL_RE.match(cell) for cell in separator):
87	                block = [lines[position], lines[position + 1]]
88	                position += 2
89	                while position < len(lines) and lines[position].strip():
90	                    block.append(lines[position])
91	                    position += 1
92	                if find_malformed_tables("\n".join(block)):
93	                    output.append(_table_to_prose(block))
94	                else:
95	                    output.extend(block)
96	                continue
97	        output.append(lines[position])
98	        position += 1
99	    repaired = "\n".join(output)
100	    remaining = find_malformed_tables(repaired)
101	    if remaining:
102	        raise ValueError(f"cleaned-output guard could not repair malformed tables: {remaining}")
103	    return repaired, defects
104

2	from __future__ import annotations
3	
4	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
5	import logging
6	import re
7	from typing import Any, Mapping, Sequence
8	from urllib.parse import urlsplit
9	
10	from src.polaris_graph.settings import resolve
11	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.rq_eligibility import (

23	_EXCLUSIVE_RE = re.compile(
24	    r"\b(?:only|exclusively|restricted\s+to|limited\s+to|must\s+(?:all\s+)?be|do\s+not\s+(?:use|cite))\b",

38	_NON_JOURNAL_DOI_REGISTRANTS = frozenset({
39	    "10.1101",   # bioRxiv / medRxiv
40	    "10.1109",   # mixed IEEE journal/conference registrant; DOI alone is ambiguous
41	    "10.1145",   # ACM proceedings-heavy registrant
42	    "10.17605",  # OSF
43	    "10.2139",   # SSRN
44	    "10.3386",   # NBER working papers
45	    "10.48550",  # arXiv

51	_NON_JOURNAL_TEXT_RE = re.compile(
52	    r"\b(?:pre[ -]?print|working[ -]?paper|discussion[ -]?paper|policy[ -]?brief|"
53	    r"press[ -]?release|government[ -]?report|white[ -]?paper|news(?:letter)?|blog)\b",
54	    re.IGNORECASE,
55	)
56	_CHROME_TITLE_RE = re.compile(
57	    r"^(?:just a moment|error\b|access denied|page not found|cookies? (?:are )?(?:disabled|turned off))",
58	    re.IGNORECASE,
59	)
60	_DOI_RE = re.compile(r"\b(10\.\d{4,9})/[\w.()/:+-]+", re.IGNORECASE)
61	
62	# Generic article-route shapes used by journal platforms.  No venue or task names are encoded.
63	_JOURNAL_ARTICLE_URL_RES = tuple(re.compile(pattern, re.IGNORECASE) for pattern in (
64	    r"/articles/pmc\d+/?$",
65	    r"/science/article/pii/",
66	    r"/journals?/[^?#]*/articles?/",
67	    r"/article/10\.\d{4,9}/",
68	    r"/doi/(?:abs/|full/|pdf/|epdf/)?10\.\d{4,9}/",
69	    r"/core/journals/[^/]+/article/",
70	    r"/(?:ojs/)?index\.php/[^/]+/article/(?:view|download)/",
71	    r"/article/\d+/\d+/",
72	    r"/article/(?:view|download)/\d+",
73	    r"/content/\d+/\d+/",
74	))
75	_JOURNAL_HOST_LABEL_RE = re.compile(r"^(?:journal|journals)[.-]", re.IGNORECASE)
76	_JOURNAL_VENUE_RE = re.compile(r"\b(?:journal|review|quarterly)\b", re.IGNORECASE)
77	_VOLUME_ISSUE_RE = re.compile(
78	    r"\b(?:vol(?:ume)?\.?\s*\d+\s*[,;:]?\s*(?:no\.?|issue)\s*\d+|\d+\s*\(\s*\d+\s*\))\b",
79	    re.IGNORECASE,
80	)
81	
82	
83	@dataclass
84	class CitationEligibilityPlan:
85	    active: bool = False
86	    body_evidence: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
87	    disclosed_evidence: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
88	    backfills: list[dict[str, str]] = field(default_factory=list)
89	
90	
91	def enabled() -> bool:
92	    return (resolve("PG_EXCLUSIVE_CITATION_ELIGIBILITY") or "").strip().lower() not in _OFF
93	
94	
95	def has_explicit_exclusive_constraint(prompt: str, constraints: Mapping[str, Any] | None) -> bool:
96	    if not constraints or not _EXCLUSIVE_RE.search(prompt or ""):
97	        return False
98	    return bool(
99	        constraints.get("source_types")
100	        or constraints.get("languages")
101	        or constraints.get("recency")
102	        or constraints.get("exclusions")
103	    )
104	
105	
106	def _host(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
107	    url = str(row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "")
108	    try:
109	        return (urlsplit(url).hostname or "").lower()
110	    except ValueError:
111	        return ""
112	
113	
114	def _host_in(host: str, choices: frozenset[str]) -> bool:
115	    return any(host == item or host.endswith("." + item) for item in choices)
116	
117	
118	def known_non_journal_surface(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> bool:
119	    """Return True only for an affirmative offline non-journal surface signal."""
120	    host = _host(row)
121	    if _host_in(host, _NON_JOURNAL_HOSTS):
122	        return True
123	    text = " ".join(str(row.get(key) or "") for key in (
124	        "title", "source_url", "url", "source_class", "document_type",
125	    ))
126	    if _NON_JOURNAL_TEXT_RE.search(text):
127	        return True
128	    doi_match = _DOI_RE.search(" ".join(str(row.get(key) or "") for key in (
129	        "doi", "source_url", "url",
130	    )))
131	    return bool(doi_match and doi_match.group(1).lower() in _NON_JOURNAL_DOI_REGISTRANTS)
132	
133	
134	def known_preprint_or_working_paper_host(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> bool:
135	    """Return True for a recognized preprint/repository/working-paper hostname."""
136	    return _host_in(_host(row), _PREPRINT_OR_WORKING_PAPER_HOSTS)
137	
138	
139	def _unknown_row_has_journal_signal(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> bool:
140	    """Conservative positive classifier for otherwise-UNKNOWN rows; pure and offline."""
141	    if known_non_journal_surface(row):
142	        return False
143	    title = str(row.get("title") or "").strip()
144	    if not title or _CHROME_TITLE_RE.search(title):
145	        return False
146	    url = str(row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "")
147	    host = _host(row)
148	
149	    # A resolvable DOI is affirmative publication metadata unless its registrant/surface is one of
150	    # the explicit repository and working-paper classes vetoed above.
151	    if _DOI_RE.search(" ".join((str(row.get("doi") or ""), url))):
152	        return True
153	    if any(pattern.search(url) for pattern in _JOURNAL_ARTICLE_URL_RES):
154	        return True
155	    if _JOURNAL_HOST_LABEL_RE.search(host + ".") and "/article" in url.lower():
156	        return True
157	
158	    venue = " ".join(str(row.get(key) or "") for key in ("journal", "venue"))
159	    publication = " ".join((title, venue, str(row.get("citation") or "")))
160	    return bool(
161	        _JOURNAL_VENUE_RE.search(venue)
162	        and _VOLUME_ISSUE_RE.search(publication)
163	    )
164	
165	
166	def _eligible(row: Mapping[str, Any], constraints: Mapping[str, Any]) -> tuple[bool, list[str]]:
167	    reasons: list[str] = []
168	    source_types = [str(item) for item in constraints.get("source_types", [])]
169	    admitted = _admitted_genres(source_types)
170	    if admitted is not None:
171	        genre = _row_genre(row)
172	        if genre == DocumentType.UNKNOWN and row.get("document_type"):
173	            try:
174	                genre = DocumentType(str(row.get("document_type")).strip().upper())
175	            except ValueError:
176	                pass
177	        if genre == DocumentType.UNKNOWN and _unknown_row_has_journal_signal(row):
178	            genre = DocumentType.JOURNAL_ARTICLE
179	        if admitted.issubset({DocumentType.JOURNAL_ARTICLE, DocumentType.REVIEW_ARTICLE}) \
180	                and known_non_journal_surface(row):
181	            # An explicit repository/working-paper/conference/dataset surface wins even over a
182	            # stale positive document_type stamp.  The published manifestation can still enter via
183	            # the same-work replacement path below; the non-journal manifestation cannot be cited.
184	            genre = DocumentType.PREPRINT
185	        if genre not in admitted:
186	            reasons.append("document_type")
187	    languages = [str(item).strip().lower()[:2] for item in constraints.get("languages", [])]
188	    if languages:
189	        language = _row_language(row)
190	        if language is None:
191	            language = detect_language_offline(" ".join(
192	                str(row.get(key) or "")
193	                for key in ("title", "snippet", "abstract", "statement", "direct_quote")
194	            ))
195	        # Missing/ambiguous language metadata is not evidence of a mismatch.  Reject only a
196	        # positively identified language outside the explicit allow-set.
197	        if language is not None and language not in languages:
198	            reasons.append("language")
199	    floor, ceiling = _parse_recency_bounds(constraints.get("recency"))
200	    if floor is not None or ceiling is not None:
201	        year = _row_year(row)
202	        if year is None or (floor is not None and year < floor) or (ceiling is not None and year > ceiling):
203	            reasons.append("recency")
204	    return not reasons, reasons
205	
206	
207	def _group_members(
208	    groups: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]] | None,
209	    rows: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]],
210	) -> dict[str, set[str]]:
211	    by_member: dict[str, set[str]] = {}
212	    for group in groups or []:
213	        members = {str(item) for item in group.get("member_evidence_ids", []) if str(item)}
214	        for member in members:
215	            by_member.setdefault(member, set()).update(members)
216	    # Composition rows can carry the same resolver result inline after canonicalization.  Consult
217	    # that representation too, rather than depending on the optional top-level group payload.
218	    for row in rows:
219	        evidence_id = str(row.get("evidence_id") or "")
220	        members = {
221	            str(item)
222	            for item in row.get("same_work_member_evidence_ids", []) or []
223	            if str(item)
224	        }
225	        if evidence_id:
226	            members.add(evidence_id)
227	        if len(members) > 1:
228	            for member in members:
229	                by_member.setdefault(member, set()).update(members)
230	    return by_member
231	
232	
233	def _title_key(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
234	    return re.sub(r"[^\w]+", " ", str(row.get("title") or "").casefold()).strip()
235	
236	
237	def plan_citation_eligibility(
238	    *,
239	    prompt: str,
240	    constraints: Mapping[str, Any] | None,
241	    evidence: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]],
242	    same_work_groups: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]] | None = None,
243	) -> CitationEligibilityPlan:
244	    original = [dict(row) for row in evidence]
245	    if not enabled() or not has_explicit_exclusive_constraint(prompt, constraints):
246	        return CitationEligibilityPlan(active=False, body_evidence=original)
247	    rules = dict(constraints or {})
248	    rows_by_id = {str(row.get("evidence_id") or ""): row for row in original}
249	    eligible_ids = {
250	        evidence_id for evidence_id, row in rows_by_id.items()
251	        if evidence_id and _eligible(row, rules)[0]
252	    }
253	    member_groups = _group_members(same_work_groups, original)
254	    eligible_by_title: dict[str, str] = {}
255	    for evidence_id in eligible_ids:
256	        key = _title_key(rows_by_id[evidence_id])
257	        if key:
258	            eligible_by_title.setdefault(key, evidence_id)
259	
260	    selected: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
261	    selected_ids: set[str] = set()
262	    disclosed: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
263	    backfills: list[dict[str, str]] = []
264	    for row in original:
265	        evidence_id = str(row.get("evidence_id") or "")
266	        ok, reasons = _eligible(row, rules)
267	        replacement = ""
268	        if not ok:
269	            candidates = member_groups.get(evidence_id, set())
270	            replacement = next((item for item in sorted(candidates) if item in eligible_ids), "")
271	            if not replacement:
272	                replacement = eligible_by_title.get(_title_key(row), "")
273	        chosen_id = evidence_id if ok else replacement
274	        if chosen_id and chosen_id not in selected_ids and chosen_id in rows_by_id:
275	            selected.append(dict(rows_by_id[chosen_id]))
276	            selected_ids.add(chosen_id)
277	        if not ok:
278	            disclosed.append({
279	                "evidence_id": evidence_id,
280	                "title": str(row.get("title") or ""),
281	                "source_url": str(row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or ""),
282	                "reasons": reasons,
283	                "statement": str(row.get("statement") or row.get("direct_quote") or ""),
284	                "eligible_same_work_evidence_id": replacement or None,
285	            })
286	            if replacement:
287	                backfills.append({"from_evidence_id": evidence_id, "to_evidence_id": replacement})
288	    logger.info(
289	        "[citation_eligibility] admitted=%d disclosed=%d backfilled=%d retained_corpus=%d",
290	        len(selected), len(disclosed), len(backfills), len(original),
291	    )
292	    return CitationEligibilityPlan(
293	        active=True,
294	        body_evidence=selected,
295	        disclosed_evidence=disclosed,
296	        backfills=backfills,
297	    )

1	"""Semantic coverage obligations carried from prompt constraints to report assembly."""
2	from __future__ import annotations
3	
4	from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass
5	import re
6	from typing import Any, Mapping, Sequence
7	
8	from src.polaris_graph.settings import resolve
9	
10	_OFF = frozenset({"", "0", "false", "no", "off", "disabled"})
11	_ROLES = ("frame", "mechanism", "cross-context comparison", "synthesis", "implication")
12	_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[^\W_]+", re.UNICODE)
13	
14	
15	@dataclass(frozen=True)
16	class CoverageObligation:
17	    concept: str
18	    role: str
19	    comparative: bool = False
20	
21	
22	def enabled() -> bool:
23	    return (resolve("PG_COVERAGE_OBLIGATIONS") or "").strip().lower() not in _OFF
24	
25	
26	def _role_for(concept: str, position: int) -> str:
27	    text = concept.lower()
28	    if re.search(r"\b(various|across|between|different|multiple)\b", text):
29	        return "cross-context comparison"
30	    if re.search(r"\b(mechanism|how|why|driver|pathway|process)\b", text):
31	        return "mechanism"
32	    if re.search(r"\b(frame|context|era|revolution|paradigm)\b", text):
33	        return "frame"
34	    if re.search(r"\b(implication|recommendation|policy|future|consequence)\b", text):
35	        return "implication"
36	    if re.search(r"\b(overall|synthesis|integrat|conclusion)\b", text):
37	        return "synthesis"
38	    return _ROLES[position % len(_ROLES)]
39	
40	
41	def build_obligations(required_coverage: Sequence[str] | None) -> list[CoverageObligation]:
42	    obligations: list[CoverageObligation] = []
43	    seen: set[str] = set()
44	    for position, raw in enumerate(required_coverage or []):
45	        concept = " ".join(str(raw).split()).strip()
46	        key = concept.casefold()
47	        if not concept or key in seen:
48	            continue
49	        seen.add(key)
50	        role = _role_for(concept, position)
51	        obligations.append(CoverageObligation(
52	            concept=concept,
53	            role=role,
54	            comparative=(role == "cross-context comparison"),
55	        ))
56	    return obligations
57	
58	
59	def thread_obligations(plans: Sequence[Any], obligations: Sequence[CoverageObligation]) -> None:
60	    """Attach each obligation to an outline focus and repeat the full spine in the conclusion."""
61	    if not enabled() or not plans or not obligations:
62	        return
63	    for position, obligation in enumerate(obligations):
64	        plan = plans[position % len(plans)]
65	        direction = (
66	            "develop one explicit cross-context comparative unit"
67	            if obligation.comparative else f"treat it analytically as a {obligation.role}"
68	        )
69	        plan.focus = (
70	            f"{plan.focus.rstrip()} Coverage obligation: connect {obligation.concept!r} to the cited "
71	            f"evidence and {direction}."
72	        )
73	    conclusion = next(
74	        (plan for plan in reversed(plans) if re.search(r"conclu|synth|implication", plan.title, re.I)),
75	        plans[-1],
76	    )
77	    spine = "; ".join(f"{item.concept} ({item.role})" for item in obligations)
78	    conclusion.focus = (
79	        f"{conclusion.focus.rstrip()} In the closing synthesis, reconnect the supported findings to "
80	        f"these prompt obligations: {spine}."
81	    )
82	
83	
84	def audit_fulfillment(
85	    obligations: Sequence[CoverageObligation],
86	    sections: Sequence[Any],
87	) -> dict[str, Any]:
88	    bodies = [str(getattr(section, "verified_text", "") or "") for section in sections]
89	    joined = " ".join(bodies).casefold()
90	    missing: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
91	    fulfilled: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
92	    for obligation in obligations:
93	        tokens = [token.casefold() for token in _WORD_RE.findall(obligation.concept)]
94	        present = bool(tokens) and all(token in joined for token in tokens)
95	        record = asdict(obligation)
96	        (fulfilled if present else missing).append(record)
97	    return {"fulfilled": fulfilled, "missing": missing}
98	
99	
100	def render_sections_preserving_outline(
101	    outline: Sequence[Any],
102	    sections: Sequence[Any],
103	) -> tuple[list[str], list[str]]:
104	    """Render every planned heading; a missing result becomes an explicit non-claim disclosure."""
105	    remaining = list(sections)
106	    rendered: list[str] = []
107	    disclosed: list[str] = []
108	    for plan in outline:
109	        match = next((section for section in remaining if section.title == plan.title), None)
110	        if match is not None:
111	            remaining.remove(match)
112	        if match is not None and not match.dropped_due_to_failure and match.verified_text:
113	            rendered.append(f"## {match.title}\n\n{match.verified_text}")
114	            continue
115	        title = str(getattr(plan, "title", "") or "Planned section")
116	        rendered.append(
117	            f"## {title}\n\nThe planned analysis could not be rendered from the available "
118	            "citation-eligible verified evidence; the gap is disclosed rather than silently omitted."
119	        )
120	        disclosed.append(title)
121	    for section in remaining:
122	        if not section.dropped_due_to_failure and section.verified_text:
123	            rendered.append(f"## {section.title}\n\n{section.verified_text}")
124	    return rendered, disclosed
125	
126	
127	def required_coverage_from(constraints: Mapping[str, Any] | None) -> list[str]:
128	    if not isinstance(constraints, Mapping):
129	        return []
130	    return [str(item) for item in constraints.get("required_coverage", []) if str(item).strip()]
